domain_note: LoCoMo measures long-term memory over two fictional characters' everyday-life conversations (e.g. 'When did Caroline attend the LGBTQ support group?'). This is lifelog memory, not developer-workflow memory. harness-mem is designed for the latter — we publish this number as a transparency reference, not as a capability claim.

methodology.metric: token-level F1 (harmonic mean of precision and recall over answer tokens)
methodology.evaluator: tests/benchmarks/locomo-evaluator.ts — tokenize + set overlap, no LLM-as-judge
methodology.retrieval: local ONNX multilingual-e5 embedding + hybrid search, no external API

iteration_log.notes: Baseline. 26,921 cache evictions due to long-conversation samples (avg ~199 QA per sample). Cache thrashed.

Cache expanded — evictions dropped to 0, F1 improved +0.0238. But absolute score still ~0.05. Domain mismatch (lifelog vs developer-workflow) is structural, not a cache problem. Pivoted to developer-domain main gate.

kumiho.note: Kumiho also targets general conversational memory and runs token-F1 on the full 1,986Q. Fair apples-to-apples score.

mempalace_raw.note: MemPalace raw mode (zero LLM) on LoCoMo top-10. Higher than harness-mem but MemPalace's explicit purpose IS general-lifelog memory.

positioning_statement: harness-mem publishes this LoCoMo full 0.0546 score on purpose. We do not tune for it — tuning would weaken our session-lifecycle, project-scoped, developer-workflow design. For apples-to-apples comparison against our actual target domain, see the developer-workflow main gate benchmarks (dev-workflow recall, bilingual recall, knowledge-update freshness, temporal ordering) published in ci-run-manifest-latest.json.
